title: YSR Aarogyasri (Andhra Pradesh) — ₹5 Lakh Free Health Insurance
meta_description: YSR Aarogyasri AP 2026 — ₹5 lakh free cashless hospitalization for 1.42 crore families. 2,434 procedures, 30 categories. Check hospitals and eligibility.

hero.one_liner: ₹5 lakh cashless hospitalization for 1.42 crore AP families — 2,434 procedures covered at government and empanelled private hospitals. No premium for eligible families.

what_is_it: YSR Aarogyasri is Andhra Pradesh's flagship health insurance scheme providing free cashless hospitalization up to ₹5 lakh per family per year. Launched in 2007 by then CM Y.S. Rajasekhara Reddy, it covers over 1.42 crore families across the state. The scheme is managed by the Dr. YSR Aarogyasri Health Care Trust under the Department of Health, Medical & Family Welfare.

The scheme covers 2,434 procedures and surgeries across 30 categories — cardiac surgery, cancer treatment, orthopedic procedures, neurological care, renal dialysis, organ transplants, burn care, and more. Treatment is cashless at empanelled government and private hospitals. You walk in with your ration card and Aadhaar, and the hospital handles everything with the trust directly.

If you're eligible for both Aarogyasri and the Central Government's Ayushman Bharat (PMJAY), you get dual coverage — up to ₹10 lakh total per year (₹5 lakh from each). Hospitals can use whichever scheme covers the specific procedure, giving AP families significantly more protection than most other states.

All families with annual income below ₹5 lakh from all sources are eligible. White ration card holders are automatically enrolled — no separate registration needed. Emergency treatment does not require pre-authorization; hospitals treat first and seek approval later. For planned surgeries, pre-authorization is needed and typically takes 1-3 days.

eligibility.label: Income
eligibility.value: Annual family income below ₹5 lakh from all sources

eligibility.label: Ration card
eligibility.value: White ration card holders automatically eligible

eligibility.label: Age
eligibility.value: No age restriction — covers all family members from newborn to elderly

eligibility.label: Coverage unit
eligibility.value: Per family, not per individual — all members covered under one card

eligibility.label: Dual coverage
eligibility.value: Can hold both Aarogyasri and Ayushman Bharat simultaneously (₹10L total)

eligibility.label: Employment
eligibility.value: No restriction — informal, formal, self-employed all eligible if income criteria met

extra_sections.heading: Major Procedure Categories Covered
rows: Bypass surgery, angioplasty, valve replacement, pacemaker
Chemotherapy, radiotherapy, tumor removal, bone marrow transplant
Joint replacement (hip, knee), fracture fixation, spinal surgery
Brain tumor removal, spine surgery, epilepsy treatment, stroke care
Kidney, liver, cornea, bone marrow transplants
Burn management, reconstructive surgery, skin grafting
Gallbladder removal, hernia repair, appendectomy, liver surgery
Lung surgery, COPD management, cochlear implant, tonsillectomy

extra_sections.caption: Emergency cases don't need pre-authorization — treatment starts immediately and authorization is obtained within 12 hours.

extra_sections.heading: How to Get Treatment Under Aarogyasri — Step by Step

steps.title: Verify hospital is empanelled
steps.description: Before visiting, confirm the hospital accepts Aarogyasri. Check the list on ysraarogyasri.ap.gov.in or call 104. Government hospitals are automatically empanelled; private hospitals must be verified.

steps.title: Go to the Aarogyasri counter at the hospital
steps.description: Every empanelled hospital has a dedicated Aarogyasri registration counter. Bring your white ration card and Aadhaar card. The staff will verify your eligibility in the system (takes 15-30 minutes).

steps.title: Doctor examines and recommends treatment
steps.description: The hospital doctor examines you and determines if your condition requires hospitalization under a covered procedure. If yes, the hospital initiates the treatment process.

steps.title: Pre-authorization for planned surgery
steps.description: For non-emergency procedures, the hospital submits a pre-authorization request to the Aarogyasri Trust. Approval typically takes 1-3 days. Emergency cases skip this — treatment starts immediately, authorization happens later.

steps.title: Receive cashless treatment
steps.description: You're admitted and treated without paying anything out of pocket. All costs up to ₹5 lakh are billed directly to the Aarogyasri Trust by the hospital.

steps.title: Discharge — no bills
steps.description: After treatment, you're discharged without paying any hospital fees. The hospital settles everything with the Trust. If the hospital asks you to pay, complain immediately to 104.

extra_sections.heading: Aarogyasri vs Ayushman Bharat — Key Differences
What is NOT Covered Under Aarogyasri
content:
OPD and consultations

Only inpatient hospitalization is covered. Doctor consultations, outpatient visits, and routine check-ups are not included.

Cosmetic procedures

Aesthetic and cosmetic surgeries are excluded. Exception: reconstructive surgery after burns or accidents is covered.

Non-empanelled hospitals

Treatment at hospitals not on the Aarogyasri network is NOT covered. Always verify empanelment before admission.

Fertility treatments

IVF, IUI, surrogacy, and other fertility procedures are excluded.

Dental and orthodontic work

Routine dental procedures, braces, and dental implants are not covered.

Motor accident cases

If your condition is covered under motor vehicle insurance (third-party or own damage), that insurance is responsible — not Aarogyasri.

Outside medicines

Medicines purchased from pharmacies outside the hospital during treatment may not be reimbursed unless specifically prescribed and approved.

extra_sections.heading: How to Check if a Hospital is Empanelled
content: The safest way: Visit ysraarogyasri.ap.gov.in and use the 'Hospital Search' feature. Enter your district and city to see all empanelled hospitals in your area.
Call the 104 helpline — the staff can verify any hospital's empanelment status over the phone. Available 24/7.
Look for visible signs at the hospital: most empanelled hospitals display the Aarogyasri empanelment certificate and have a dedicated Aarogyasri counter near the entrance.

Government hospitals in AP are automatically empanelled. For private hospitals, ALWAYS verify before admission — not all private hospitals participate, and treatment at non-empanelled hospitals will not be covered.

extra_sections.heading: ₹5 lakh cover per family per year
extra_sections.content: YSR Aarogyasri covers 2,446 medical procedures including surgeries, therapies, and diagnostics. The ₹5 lakh limit is per family per year. For critical illnesses like cancer, kidney transplant, and cardiac surgery, an additional ₹5 lakh (total ₹10 lakh) is available under the enhanced package.

extra_sections.heading: Works with Ayushman Bharat
extra_sections.content: YSR Aarogyasri is integrated with PM-JAY (Ayushman Bharat) in Andhra Pradesh. If you have both cards, the hospital uses whichever scheme covers your specific treatment. The AP government tops up the Ayushman ₹5 lakh with additional state funds for procedures not covered under PM-JAY.

how_to_apply.title: Check if you're already enrolled
how_to_apply.description: White ration card holders are automatically eligible. Visit ysraarogyasri.ap.gov.in or call 104 with your ration card number to check your status.

how_to_apply.title: If not enrolled — visit nearest government hospital or Aarogyasri center
how_to_apply.description: Bring your ration card (white or pink), Aadhaar card, and income proof. The Aarogyasri counter staff will verify your eligibility in the system.

how_to_apply.title: Submit documents for registration
how_to_apply.description: If eligible, provide your ration card, Aadhaar, and a passport photo. Registration is typically processed on the spot at government hospitals.

how_to_apply.title: Receive Aarogyasri Health Card
how_to_apply.description: You'll get an Aarogyasri card or digital confirmation. Keep this safe — you'll need it every time you seek treatment at an empanelled hospital.

how_to_apply.title: Use at any empanelled hospital across AP
how_to_apply.description: Present your Aarogyasri card and Aadhaar at any empanelled hospital in AP. The hospital handles the rest — verification, pre-authorization, and billing directly with the Trust.

important_alert.text: Always verify hospital empanelment before planned treatment. Non-empanelled hospitals cannot bill the Aarogyasri Trust, and you'll have to pay out of pocket. Check at ysraarogyasri.ap.gov.in or call 104. For emergencies, go to the nearest government hospital — all government hospitals in AP are empanelled.

faqs.question: I have Ayushman Bharat. Do I still need Aarogyasri?
faqs.answer: If you're in AP with income below ₹5 lakh, you likely qualify for both. Having both gives you ₹10 lakh combined annual coverage. Hospitals can bill either scheme depending on which covers the procedure. There's no downside to having both.

faqs.question: Do I need to pay anything at the hospital?
faqs.answer: Nothing. Treatment is completely cashless at empanelled hospitals. If any hospital demands payment from you for a covered procedure, report it immediately to the 104 helpline. The hospital settles everything directly with the Trust.

faqs.question: What if my bill exceeds ₹5 lakh?
faqs.answer: Aarogyasri covers up to ₹5 lakh per year. If your treatment costs more (rare for single procedures), the excess is your responsibility — unless you also have Ayushman Bharat, in which case the overflow can be covered under that scheme (additional ₹5L).

faqs.question: Can I use Aarogyasri outside Andhra Pradesh?
faqs.answer: No. Aarogyasri is AP-specific and works only at empanelled hospitals within the state. For treatment outside AP, use your Ayushman Bharat card instead (if you have one), as that's a national scheme.

faqs.question: Is emergency treatment covered without pre-authorization?
faqs.answer: Yes. In emergencies, go to the nearest empanelled or government hospital immediately. Treatment starts right away. Pre-authorization is sought by the hospital afterward. Never delay emergency care for paperwork.

faqs.question: How do I check my eligibility or Aarogyasri card status?
faqs.answer: Visit ysraarogyasri.ap.gov.in → use 'Check Eligibility' with your ration card number. Or call 104. The fastest way: visit any empanelled hospital's Aarogyasri counter with your ration card — they'll check on the spot.

faqs.question: My family member needs dialysis. Is it covered?
faqs.answer: Yes. Renal dialysis is covered under Aarogyasri's renal care category. Multiple dialysis sessions can be authorized within the ₹5 lakh annual limit. Pre-authorization is required for planned dialysis schedules. Visit an empanelled hospital with your Aarogyasri card.

faqs.question: What if the hospital I want to go to is not empanelled?
faqs.answer: Treatment at non-empanelled hospitals is NOT covered. You'll pay full costs yourself. Before planned treatment, always verify empanelment at ysraarogyasri.ap.gov.in or call 104. For emergencies, go to the nearest government hospital — all are empanelled.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for YSR Aarogyasri?
faqs.answer: All families with annual income below ₹5 lakh in Andhra Pradesh are eligible. White ration card holders are automatically enrolled. The family must have an Aarogyasri Health Card issued by the AP government. Apply through village/ward secretariat or Mee Seva centers with ration card, Aadhaar, and family photo.

faqs.question: Can I use YSR Aarogyasri outside Andhra Pradesh?
faqs.answer: Yes. YSR Aarogyasri is valid at empanelled hospitals across India — including major hospitals in Hyderabad, Chennai, Bangalore, and Delhi. Over 1,400 hospitals are empanelled nationwide. Check the hospital list at aarogyasri.telangana.gov.in or call the helpline 104 before travelling for treatment.

ministry: Department of Health, Medical & Family Welfare, Govt of Andhra Pradesh
